system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,omega)  Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

RAS

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: `log.blockMesh` — Unknown blockVertex type

**Error Message:**
```
Unknown blockVertex type v0
Valid blockVertex types are
3
(
name
point
project
)
```

This error occurs because the `blockMeshDict` references vertices (`v0`, `v1`, ..., `v7`) that don't exist in the current version of OpenFOAM being used. This suggests the **OpenFOAM version has changed its vertex definition format**.

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

```
vertices
(
    (-20.6 0 -0.5)    v0
    (0 0 -0.5)      v1
    ...
);
```

In newer versions of OpenFOAM (particularly OpenFOAM Foundation versions like openfoam.org v2012+, v2106, v2206, v2312, v2406, etc.), the vertex format changed. Instead of defining named vertices (`v0`, `v1`, ...) inline with their coordinates, the standard format now expects just the coordinate lists, and the vertex naming is handled internally.

## Root Cause

The syntax `(-20.6 0 -0.5)    v0` is the **old-style** vertex definition format. In modern OpenFOAM (Foundation versions), vertices are defined using only the coordinate syntax without explicit labels:

```
vertices
(
    (-20.6 0 -0.5)
    (0 0 -0.5)
    (0 25.4 -0.5)
    (20.6 0 -0.5)
    (-20.6 0 0.5)
    (0 0 0.5)
    (0 25.4 0.5)
    (20.6 0 0.5)
);
```

The old style with `v0`, `v1`, etc., is still supported in some older/community versions but fails in others.

## Solution

**Modify `system/blockMeshDict`** — Remove the vertex labels (`v0`, `v1`, ..., `v7`) from the vertex definitions. Keep only the coordinate tuples:

Change:
```
vertices
(
    (-20.6 0 -0.5)    v0
    (0 0 -0.5)      v1
    (0 25.4 -0.5)   v2
    (20.6 0 -0.5)   v3
    (-20.6 0 0.5)   v4
    (0 0 0.5)       v5
    (0 25.4 0.5)    v6
    (20.6 0 0.5)    v7
);
```

To:
```
vertices
(
    (-20.6 0 -0.5)
    (0 0 -0.5)
    (0 25.4 -0.5)
    (20.6 0 -0.5)
    (-20.6 0 0.5)
    (0 0 0.5)
    (0 25.4 0.5)
    (20.6 0 0.5)
);
```

Keep the rest of the `blockMeshDict` unchanged (the block definitions, boundary assignments, edges, mergePatchPairs).

After fixing the `blockMeshDict`, re-run `blockMesh` followed by `pimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Remove vertex labels v0 through v7 from the vertices list, keeping only the coordinate tuples; Change vertices from format with labels to format without labels'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
